program.name: Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP)
program.description:
CHIP gives free or low-cost health coverage to children under 19. It pays for doctor visits, dental and vision care, hospital stays, and prescriptions.

In Kansas, CHIP is part of KanCare. If your child qualifies, you pick a KanCare health plan that covers their care.

CHIP is for families who earn too much for Medicaid but still need help paying for coverage. Depending on your income, you may pay a small monthly premium.

You apply for CHIP through KanCare. The same form also checks if your child can get Medicaid.
program.description_short: Free or low-cost health coverage for kids under 19
program.website_description: Free or low-cost health coverage for kids under 19

documents.text: Proof of identity for the child (ex: birth certificate, school ID)
Proof of unearned income, if any (ex: child support, Social Security, unemployment, or pension)
Proof of citizenship or immigration status (ex: birth certificate, passport, immigration documents)

navigators.name: Healthy Kansas Kids (GraceMed Health Clinic)
navigators.description: Free help applying for or renewing KanCare (Medicaid and CHIP) for children, families, and pregnant women. Run by GraceMed Health Clinic — get help by phone, at a clinic, or at a community event across Kansas.
